creator: tech-lists@zyxst.net
text:
(In reply to shamsher from comment #5)

Hi, I cannot reproduce your problem. It looks like your connection is dropping data for ntp. Why are you using ntp from ports instead of base? do you or your ISP block any ports outbound?

output from a machine on my network that's behind NAT. system is stable/13 and ntpdate is 4.2.8p15-a as is ntpd

in my /etc/rc.conf there's this:

ntpdate_enable="YES"
ntpdate_hosts="uk.pool.ntp.org"
ntpd_enable="YES"

output:

% ntpdate -d 0.freebsd.pool.ntp.org
